# GPU Develop — Plan 3b: Offscreen GPU Export (Phase 6)

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Export through the **same WebGL2 shader** the preview uses (single source of truth), so the exported file is generated by the GPU invert+finish pipeline instead of the parallel CPU `invert_image`/`finish_image` — eliminating the dual-implementation drift risk. Preview and export already heal pre-invert (Plan 3a), so they match end to end.

**Architecture:** Rust decodes full-res, runs `bake_working` (geometry + pre-invert dust/IR heal — the Plan 3a helper) on the **full-res** image, stashes the half-float bytes + resolves uniforms, and returns dims. The frontend uploads that one full-res texture, runs the invert→finish two-pass into an **offscreen FBO** (RGBA8 for 8-bit formats, RGBA16F for 16-bit), `readPixels` back, and ships the result to Rust, which builds an `Image` and encodes with the **existing** encoders. Images whose long edge exceeds `gl.MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE`, or when WebGL2 is unavailable, **fall back to the unchanged CPU `export_image`** (no tiling in v1).

**Tech Stack:** Rust (Tauri 2, film-core, `half`), WebGL2 (FBO + readPixels, EXT_color_buffer_float), TypeScript, Svelte.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-04-gpu-develop-pipeline-design.md` (Phase 6). Builds on Plan 2 (renderer two-pass, `setSourceFloat`, `setInversion`) and Plan 3a (`bake_working`, `resolve_to_uniforms`).

---

## Context the implementer needs

- Current CPU export: `export_image(id, params, out_path, image_crop, rot90, flip_h, flip_v, angle, dust, ir_removal, format, meta_override, session)` (`commands.rs`) decodes full-res via `decode_any`, applies `orient`/`rotate`/`crop`, `resolve_params`, `invert_image`, `dust::apply`/`apply_ir` (post-invert today), `finish_image`, then encodes. **This command stays as the fallback.**
- `bake_working(&Image, &BakeSpec) -> Image` (Plan 3a, `gpu_upload.rs`) applies geometry + **pre-invert** heal and does NOT cap resolution (the cap lives in `pack_rgba16f`). Reuse it on the full-res decoded image.
- `resolve_to_uniforms(&InvertParams, base) -> ResolvedInversion` (`gpu_upload.rs`). Export uses `dev.base` (the sampled film base).
- `pack_rgba16f(&Image, cap)` — pass `u32::MAX` for export to avoid downscaling.
- Encoders (`encode.rs` + `film_core::export::write_tiff16`): `write_jpeg(&Image, path, quality, max_bytes)`, `write_png(&Image, path, bits)`, `write_tiff8(&Image, path)`, `write_tiff16(&Image, path)`. All take `&film_core::Image { width, height, pixels: Vec<[f32;3]>, ir }`. Build an `Image` from readback.
- `ExportFormat { kind: String /* "jpeg"|"tiff"|"png" */, bit_depth: u8, quality: u8, max_bytes: Option<u64> }`.
- Renderer (Plan 2): `setSourceFloat`, `setInversion`, `setGeometry`, two-pass `draw()` to the canvas. We add an **export render to an offscreen FBO + readPixels** path. The `sourceUV` y-flip (`uv.y = 1.0 - uv.y`) means readback rows come out **top-down** (image row 0 first) — matching `Image` row-major; verify in Task 6.
- Finishing uniforms/LUT/grade are computed frontend-side: `finishUniforms(params)`, `toneLutBytes(params)`, `colorGrade(params)`.

## File Structure

```
app/src-tauri/src/
├── session.rs       ADD: Session.pending_export: Mutex<Option<PreparedExport>>
├── gpu_upload.rs    ADD: image_from_rgba8(w,h,&[u8]) + image_from_rgba_f32(w,h,&[f32]) -> Image
├── commands.rs      ADD: export_begin, export_pixels, export_finish commands; keep export_image as fallback
└── lib.rs           register the 3 new commands

app/src/lib/viewport/gl/
└── renderer.ts      ADD: renderExport(src, w, h, inv, finishUniforms, lut, cg, bit16) -> {data, w, h}
                     (offscreen FBO render of invert+finish, identity geometry, readPixels)

app/src/lib/
├── api.ts           ADD: exportBegin, exportPixels, exportFinish bindings
└── (export caller)  the code that currently calls api.exportImage: try GPU path, else CPU fallback
```

## Task 1: Rust — build an `Image` from readback bytes

**Files:** `app/src-tauri/src/gpu_upload.rs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Failing test**

Add to `gpu_upload.rs` tests:

```rust
    #[test]
    fn image_from_rgba8_drops_alpha_and_normalizes() {
        // 1x2 image, RGBA bytes; alpha ignored, channels /255 into f32.
        let bytes = [255u8, 128, 0, 255,   0, 64, 255, 255];
        let img = image_from_rgba8(1, 2, &bytes);
        assert_eq!((img.width, img.height), (1, 2));
        assert!((img.pixels[0][0] - 1.0).abs() < 1e-3);
        assert!((img.pixels[0][1] - 128.0 / 255.0).abs() < 1e-3);
        assert!((img.pixels[0][2] - 0.0).abs() < 1e-3);
        assert!((img.pixels[1][1] - 64.0 / 255.0).abs() < 1e-3);
    }

    #[test]
    fn image_from_rgba_f32_drops_alpha() {
        let data = [0.25f32, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0,   0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 1.0];
        let img = image_from_rgba_f32(2, 1, &data);
        assert_eq!((img.width, img.height), (2, 1));
        assert_eq!(img.pixels[0], [0.25, 0.5, 0.75]);
        assert_eq!(img.pixels[1], [0.1, 0.2, 0.3]);
    }
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run → fails (not defined)**

Run: `cargo test --manifest-path app/src-tauri/Cargo.toml gpu_upload::tests::image_from`
Expected: FAIL to compile.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement**

```rust
/// Build a linear-RGB Image from tightly-packed RGBA8 readback (alpha dropped, /255).
pub fn image_from_rgba8(w: u32, h: u32, bytes: &[u8]) -> Image {
    let n = (w * h) as usize;
    let mut pixels = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for i in 0..n {
        let o = i * 4;
        pixels.push([bytes[o] as f32 / 255.0, bytes[o + 1] as f32 / 255.0, bytes[o + 2] as f32 / 255.0]);
    }
    Image { width: w as usize, height: h as usize, pixels, ir: None }
}

/// Build a linear-RGB Image from tightly-packed RGBA f32 readback (alpha dropped).
pub fn image_from_rgba_f32(w: u32, h: u32, data: &[f32]) -> Image {
    let n = (w * h) as usize;
    let mut pixels = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    for i in 0..n {
        let o = i * 4;
        pixels.push([data[o], data[o + 1], data[o + 2]]);
    }
    Image { width: w as usize, height: h as usize, pixels, ir: None }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run → passes**

Run: `cargo test --manifest-path app/src-tauri/Cargo.toml gpu_upload::tests::image_from`
Expected: PASS (both).

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add app/src-tauri/src/gpu_upload.rs
git commit -m "feat(gpu): image_from_rgba8/f32 — build Image from GPU readback"
```

## Task 2: Rust — session-held prepared export buffer

**Files:** `app/src-tauri/src/session.rs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the field + type**

In `session.rs`, add:

```rust
/// A full-res baked (geometry + pre-invert heal) raw-negative buffer awaiting GPU
/// export, plus the dims the frontend uploads at. Held between export_begin and
/// export_pixels so the file is decoded+baked exactly once per export.
pub struct PreparedExport {
    pub w: u32,
    pub h: u32,
    pub bytes: Vec<u8>, // half-float RGBA, full-res
}
```

Add to `Session`:

```rust
    pub pending_export: Mutex<Option<PreparedExport>>,
```

If `Session` derives `Default`, `Mutex<Option<_>>` is `Default` — fine. If it's constructed explicitly somewhere, add `pending_export: Mutex::new(None)` there.

## Task 3: Rust — `export_begin` / `export_pixels` / `export_finish`

**Files:** `app/src-tauri/src/commands.rs`, `app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the three commands**

In `commands.rs`:

```rust
use crate::gpu_upload::{bake_working, image_from_rgba8, image_from_rgba_f32, pack_rgba16f, resolve_to_uniforms, BakeSpec, ResolvedInversion};
use crate::session::PreparedExport;

#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]
pub struct ExportPrep {
    pub w: u32,
    pub h: u32,
    pub uniforms: ResolvedInversion,
}

/// Decode full-res, bake geometry + pre-invert heal, stash the half-float bytes,
/// and return the dims + resolved inversion uniforms. The frontend then renders
/// the GPU invert+finish offscreen and calls export_finish with the readback.
#[tauri::command]
pub fn export_begin(id: String, params: InvertParams, spec: BakeSpec, session: State<Session>) -> Result<ExportPrep, String> {
    ensure_resident(&session, &id)?;
    let (path, base) = {
        let images = session.images.lock().unwrap();
        let img = images.get(&id).ok_or("unknown image id")?;
        let dev = img.developed.as_ref().ok_or("not developed")?;
        (img.path.clone(), dev.base)
    };
    let full = decode_any(Path::new(&path))?;
    let baked = bake_working(&full, &spec);           // geometry + pre-invert heal, full-res
    let (w, h, bytes) = pack_rgba16f(&baked, u32::MAX); // no cap for export
    let uniforms = resolve_to_uniforms(&params, base);
    *session.pending_export.lock().unwrap() = Some(PreparedExport { w, h, bytes });
    Ok(ExportPrep { w, h, uniforms })
}

/// Return the stashed half-float bytes for upload (consumes nothing; kept until export_finish).
#[tauri::command]
pub fn export_pixels(session: State<Session>) -> Result<tauri::ipc::Response, String> {
    let guard = session.pending_export.lock().unwrap();
    let prep = guard.as_ref().ok_or("no prepared export")?;
    Ok(tauri::ipc::Response::new(prep.bytes.clone()))
}

/// `bit16 = true` → `data` is f32 RGBA (4 floats/px); else RGBA8 (4 bytes/px).
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
pub struct ExportReadback {
    pub w: u32,
    pub h: u32,
    pub bit16: bool,
}

/// Build an Image from the GPU readback and encode it with the chosen format.
#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
#[tauri::command]
pub fn export_finish(
    id: String,
    out_path: String,
    readback: ExportReadback,
    data: Vec<u8>,
    format: ExportFormat,
    meta_override: Option<MetaOverride>,
    session: State<Session>,
) -> Result<(), String> {
    let img = if readback.bit16 {
        // data is little-endian f32 RGBA
        let floats: Vec<f32> = data
            .chunks_exact(4)
            .map(|b| f32::from_le_bytes([b[0], b[1], b[2], b[3]]))
            .collect();
        image_from_rgba_f32(readback.w, readback.h, &floats)
    } else {
        image_from_rgba8(readback.w, readback.h, &data)
    };
    let out = Path::new(&out_path);
    match format.kind.as_str() {
        "tiff" => {
            if format.bit_depth == 16 { film_core::export::write_tiff16(&img, out).map_err(|e| format!("{e}")) }
            else { write_tiff8(&img, out) }
        }
        "png" => write_png(&img, out, format.bit_depth),
        "jpeg" => write_jpeg(&img, out, format.quality, format.max_bytes),
        other => Err(format!("unknown export format: {other}")),
    }?;
    // EXIF, mirroring export_image.
    let metadata = {
        let images = session.images.lock().unwrap();
        images.get(&id).map(|i| i.metadata.clone())
    };
    if let Some(md) = metadata {
        let eff = effective_metadata(&md, meta_override.as_ref());
        if let Err(e) = crate::exif_write::write_exif(out, &eff) { eprintln!("[exif] embed failed: {e}"); }
    }
    *session.pending_export.lock().unwrap() = None; // release the buffer
    Ok(())
}
```

(Match `effective_metadata`/`MetaOverride`/`write_*` exactly to how `export_image` calls them — copy the format `match` + EXIF block from `export_image` so the two paths encode identically.)

Add `export_begin, export_pixels, export_finish` to `generate_handler!`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Build**

Expected: clean.

## Task 4: TS — renderer offscreen export render + readback

**Files:** `app/src/lib/viewport/gl/renderer.ts`

No unit test (WebGL); verified by build + the pixel-match E2E in Task 6.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add `renderExport`**

Add a method to `FinishRenderer` that renders the full invert+finish to an offscreen FBO at the given size with identity geometry, and reads it back. Use a dedicated export FBO/textures so it doesn't disturb the live preview state:

```ts
  /**
   * Render invert+finish for EXPORT into an offscreen FBO at (w,h) and read back.
   * bit16=false → returns RGBA8 (Uint8Array); bit16=true → RGBA f32 (Float32Array).
   * Geometry is identity (Rust already baked orient/crop/heal into `src`).
   */
  renderExport(
    src: Uint16Array, w: number, h: number,
    inv: InversionUniforms,
    fu: FinishUniforms, lut: Uint8Array, cg: ColorGradeUniforms,
    bit16: boolean,
  ): { data: Uint8Array | Float32Array; w: number; h: number } | null {
    const gl = this.gl; if (!gl || !this.invProg || !this.prog) return null;
    if (w > gl.getParameter(gl.MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE) || h > gl.getParameter(gl.MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE)) return null;

    // Upload the full-res source, set inversion + identity geometry + finishing.
    this.setSourceFloat(src, w, h);
    this.setInversion(inv);
    this.setGeometry({ crop_off: [0, 0], crop_scale: [1, 1], angle: 0, orient: [1, 0, 0, 1], raw: false, outW: w, outH: h });
    this.setUniforms(fu); this.setLut(lut); this.setColorGrade(cg);

    // PASS 1 → interTex (RGBA16F) via the existing draw() invert path is reused, but we
    // need PASS 2 to land in an offscreen, readable buffer instead of the canvas.
    // Reuse draw() for PASS 1 by binding the inter FBO, then run PASS 2 manually here.
    // Simpler: run the full two-pass into an export FBO.
    const outInternal = bit16 ? gl.RGBA16F : gl.RGBA8;
    const outFmt = gl.RGBA;
    const outType = bit16 ? gl.HALF_FLOAT : gl.UNSIGNED_BYTE;

    // Export output texture + FBO.
    const outTex = gl.createTexture();
    gl.bindTexture(gl.TEXTURE_2D, outTex);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_MIN_FILTER, gl.NEAREST);
    gl.texParameteri(gl.TEXTURE_2D, gl.TEXTURE_MAG_FILTER, gl.NEAREST);
    gl.texImage2D(gl.TEXTURE_2D, 0, outInternal, w, h, 0, outFmt, outType, null);
    const outFbo = gl.createFramebuffer();

    // PASS 1: invert → interTex (draw() already does this when called; but draw() also
    // does PASS 2 to the canvas). To avoid the canvas pass, invoke the invert pass via a
    // small private helper. If draw() is structured as drawInvert()+drawFinish(), call
    // drawInvert() here; otherwise refactor draw() to expose them. (See Step 2.)
    this.drawInvertPass();

    // PASS 2: finish interTex → outTex.
    gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, outFbo);
    gl.framebufferTexture2D(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, gl.COLOR_ATTACHMENT0, gl.TEXTURE_2D, outTex, 0);
    this.drawFinishPass(w, h);
    // Read back.
    let data: Uint8Array | Float32Array;
    if (bit16) { data = new Float32Array(w * h * 4); gl.readPixels(0, 0, w, h, gl.RGBA, gl.FLOAT, data); }
    else { data = new Uint8Array(w * h * 4); gl.readPixels(0, 0, w, h, gl.RGBA, gl.UNSIGNED_BYTE, data); }
    gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, null);
    gl.deleteFramebuffer(outFbo); gl.deleteTexture(outTex);
    return { data, w, h };
  }
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Refactor `draw()` into `drawInvertPass()` + `drawFinishPass(w,h)`**

Split the existing `draw()` so the invert pass and finish pass are callable independently (the live `draw()` calls both into the canvas; `renderExport` calls invert then finish-into-FBO). Extract:
- `private drawInvertPass()` — the PASS 1 block from `draw()` (binds `this.fbo`/`interTex`, sets invert+geometry uniforms, draws). Unchanged logic.
- `private drawFinishPass(vw: number, vh: number)` — the PASS 2 block, but `gl.viewport(0,0,vw,vh)` and it draws to **whatever framebuffer is currently bound** (canvas for live, `outFbo` for export). For live `draw()`, bind canvas (`bindFramebuffer(null)`) before calling it.
Rewrite `draw()` as: `if (useFloat && inv) this.drawInvertPass(); gl.bindFramebuffer(gl.FRAMEBUFFER, null); this.drawFinishPass(this.srcW, this.srcH);`

Keep all existing behavior identical for the live path (verify preview still works in Task 6).

- [ ] **Step 3: Build**

Run: `cd app && npm run build`
Expected: clean (TypeScript happy; `bit16` float readback requires `EXT_color_buffer_float`, already required by the renderer).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add app/src/lib/viewport/gl/renderer.ts
git commit -m "feat(gpu): renderExport — offscreen invert+finish + readPixels"
```

## Task 5: TS — api bindings + the export caller (GPU path with CPU fallback)

**Files:** `app/src/lib/api.ts`, and the component that currently calls `api.exportImage`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add api bindings**

```ts
  exportBegin: (id: string, params: InvertParams, spec: BakeSpec) =>
    invoke<{ w: number; h: number; uniforms: import("./viewport/gl/invert").ResolvedInversion }>(
      "export_begin", { id, params, spec: { ...spec, dust: wireDust(spec.dust) } }),
  exportPixels: () => invoke<ArrayBuffer>("export_pixels"),
  exportFinish: (id: string, outPath: string, readback: { w: number; h: number; bit16: boolean },
                 data: number[] | Uint8Array, format: ExportFormat, metaOverride: MetaOverride | null) =>
    invoke<void>("export_finish", { id, outPath, readback, data, format, metaOverride }),
```

(`data` must serialize as a byte array Tauri accepts — pass `Array.from(new Uint8Array(buffer))` or the typed array per the project's invoke convention; confirm against how other byte args are passed.)

- [ ] **Step 2: Wire the export caller to try GPU first, fall back to CPU**

Where the app currently calls `api.exportImage(...)`, add a GPU attempt that falls back. Build the `spec` (BakeSpec) and `finishing` inputs from the same params/geometry the CPU export used:

```ts
async function exportOne(id, params, outPath, geo, dust, irRemoval, format, metaOverride, renderer) {
  const bit16 = (format.kind === "tiff" || format.kind === "png") && format.bit_depth === 16;
  const spec = { rot90: geo.rot90, flip_h: geo.flipH, flip_v: geo.flipV, angle: geo.angle,
                 image_crop: geo.imageCrop, dust, ir_removal: irRemoval };
  if (renderer) {
    try {
      const prep = await api.exportBegin(id, params, spec);
      const maxTex = renderer.maxTextureSize();           // add a tiny getter (Step 3)
      if (prep.w <= maxTex && prep.h <= maxTex) {
        const buf = await api.exportPixels();
        const out = renderer.renderExport(
          new Uint16Array(buf), prep.w, prep.h,
          toInversionUniforms(prep.uniforms),
          finishUniforms(params), toneLutBytes(params), colorGrade(params), bit16);
        if (out) {
          const bytes = bit16
            ? new Uint8Array((out.data as Float32Array).buffer)
            : (out.data as Uint8Array);
          await api.exportFinish(id, outPath, { w: out.w, h: out.h, bit16 },
            Array.from(bytes), format, metaOverride ?? null);
          return; // GPU export done
        }
      }
    } catch (e) { console.warn("GPU export failed, falling back to CPU:", e); }
  }
  // Fallback: unchanged CPU export.
  await api.exportImage(id, params, outPath, geo.imageCrop, geo.rot90, geo.flipH, geo.flipV,
    geo.angle, dust, irRemoval, format, metaOverride);
}
```

(Adapt the exact arg names to the real `exportImage` signature in `api.ts` and the real call site. The key invariant: **GPU path on success, CPU path on any failure / oversize / no-renderer.**)

- [ ] **Step 3: Add `maxTextureSize()` getter to the renderer**

```ts
  maxTextureSize(): number { return this.gl ? this.gl.getParameter(this.gl.MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE) : 0; }
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Build**

Run: `cd app && npm run build`
Expected: clean.

## Task 6: MANUAL E2E + pixel-match validation (the critical gate)

**Files:** `docs/superpowers/poc-findings.md`

GPU export correctness is only fully verifiable by comparing output to the trusted CPU export.

- [ ] **Step 1: Preview regression check**

`cd app && npm run tauri dev`. Confirm the **live preview still works** (the `draw()` refactor didn't break it): inverted image, exposure/temp/tint instant, dust/IR, zoom. If broken, the `drawInvertPass`/`drawFinishPass` split regressed — fix before continuing.

- [ ] **Step 2: GPU export a JPEG (8-bit) and an 8-bit PNG**

Export a developed image as JPEG and as 8-bit PNG. Confirm the files are written and look correct (correctly inverted, finished, oriented, dust-healed).

- [ ] **Step 3: Pixel-match vs CPU export**

Temporarily force the CPU fallback (e.g. comment out the GPU branch) and export the SAME image+params to a second file. Compare GPU vs CPU output (any image diff tool, or a quick script). Expect them to match within a small tolerance (≤ a few levels of 8-bit, from f16 vs f32 + GLSL `log2` vs `log10` rounding). Large/structural differences = a real bug (matrix order, gamma, heal domain) — investigate before shipping.

- [ ] **Step 4: 16-bit TIFF/PNG**

Export a 16-bit TIFF (`bit16` float readback path). Confirm it opens and looks correct. If float readback is unsupported on the device, the `renderExport` guard returns null → CPU fallback (acceptable; note it).

- [ ] **Step 5: Oversize fallback**

If you have a scan whose long edge > `MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE` (usually 16384), confirm it falls back to CPU export cleanly. Otherwise note this path is untested on available files.

- [ ] **Step 6: Record results**

Append a dated `Plan 3b results` section to `poc-findings.md`: GPU export landed; pixel-match vs CPU export result (the key number); which formats GPU-rendered vs fell back; preview-regression check passed. Commit:

```bash
git add docs/superpowers/poc-findings.md
git commit -m "docs(gpu): record Plan 3b GPU-export results + CPU pixel-match"
```

---

## Self-Review

- **Spec coverage:** Phase 6 (offscreen GPU export, single source of truth) → Task 1 (Image from readback), Task 2 (handoff buffer), Task 3 (begin/pixels/finish, reusing `bake_working` so export heals pre-invert exactly like preview), Task 4 (offscreen render + readPixels), Task 5 (GPU-with-CPU-fallback caller), Task 6 (pixel-match validation). The CPU `export_image` is retained as the fallback (oversize / no-GL / 16-bit-unsupported), so nothing regresses.
- **Decisions & scope limits (stated, not hidden):** (1) **No tiling in v1** — images with a dimension > `gl.MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE` fall back to CPU export; tiling is a future extension. (2) **Big IPC** — full-res half-float in (`export_pixels`) + full-res readback out (`export_finish`) is heavy (hundreds of MB on large scans); acceptable for a one-shot, non-interactive export, flagged. (3) **8-bit readback for 8-bit formats, f32 for 16-bit** — keeps the common JPEG path light. (4) The preview-vs-export **texture/unsharp radius** difference is pre-existing (radius is per-resolution on both CPU and GPU) and out of scope; GPU export still matches CPU export because both run at full res.
- **Placeholder scan:** No TBD/TODO. `renderExport` references `drawInvertPass`/`drawFinishPass` which Task 4 Step 2 defines (the `draw()` refactor). The export-caller arg names are explicitly flagged to match the real `exportImage` signature. Manual-E2E steps list expected results.
- **Type consistency:** `ExportPrep { w, h, uniforms: ResolvedInversion }` and `ExportReadback { w, h, bit16 }` consistent Rust↔TS; `renderExport` signature matches its caller in Task 5; `image_from_rgba8/f32` names match their use in `export_finish`; `PreparedExport` fields (`w,h,bytes`) consistent between Task 2 and Task 3.
- **Risk (restated honestly):** this is the highest-risk plan in the project for the least user-visible gain — the CPU export already works and matches the shader. The value is single-source-of-truth + heal-domain consistency. Task 6 Step 3 (pixel-match) is the gate; if it doesn't match within tolerance, prefer keeping CPU export as the default and treat GPU export as opt-in until reconciled.
```
